- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

content.content:
1	"""Tiered reasoning seam — the single abstraction over LLM execution by stakes tier.
2	
3	No engine component talks to an LLM directly; everything routes through :class:`ReasoningSeam`.
4	Ported and generalized from the prior platform's hardened ``reasoning_client`` (ARCHITECTURE.md
5	§1, ADR-0003). Two execution tiers:
6	
7	- ``StakesTier.BULK`` — a local model runtime (loopback-only, e.g. Ollama) for high-volume,
8	  low-stakes work.
9	- ``StakesTier.HIGH_STAKES`` — a frontier model via a **fixed-cost subscription** path
10	  (the ``claude`` CLI / Agent SDK using an OAuth token). No metered API key is ever used:
11	  every metered Anthropic credential is scrubbed from the subprocess environment first.
12	
13	Hardened invariants preserved from the prior platform:
14	
15	- forward-proof credential scrubbing (any ``ANTHROPIC_*KEY``/``*TOKEN`` stripped; the
16	  subscription ``CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`` preserved);
17	- hard per-call timeouts via a bounded worker pool with a saturation guard;
18	- independent retry budgets — exponential backoff for transient errors, long backpressure
19	  for rate limits — and permanent errors never retried;
20	- robust JSON extraction via a balanced-brace walker (not a greedy regex);
21	- loopback enforcement on the local backend (ADR-0003).
22	
23	Heavy backends (``langchain_ollama``, ``claude_agent_sdk``) and tracing (``opentelemetry``)
24	are imported lazily, so this module imports — and is unit-testable by mocking ``_attempt`` —
25	with none of them installed. Install the optional ``reasoning`` extra to run real calls.
26	"""
27	
28	from __future__ import annotations
29	
30	import atexit
31	import json
32	import os
33	import threading
34	import time
35	from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor
36	from concurrent.futures import TimeoutError as FuturesTimeout
37	from contextlib import contextmanager
38	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
39	from enum import Enum
40	from pathlib import Path
41	from typing import Any, Callable, Iterator, Mapping, Protocol, cast
42	
43	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping
44	from assay_engine._local import require_loopback_url
45	
46	
47	class StakesTier(Enum):
48	    BULK = "bulk"  # local model — high volume, low stakes
49	    HIGH_STAKES = "high"  # frontier model via fixed-cost subscription — gated, traced
50	
51	
52	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
53	class ReasoningRequest:
54	    prompt: str
55	    tier: StakesTier
56	    purpose: str
57	    params: Mapping[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
58	
59	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
60	        object.__setattr__(self, "params", freeze_mapping(self.params))
61	
62	
63	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
64	# Errors
65	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
66	class ReasoningError(RuntimeError):
67	    """Transient reasoning failure (eligible for retry)."""
68	
69	
70	class PermanentReasoningError(ReasoningError):
71	    """Non-transient failure — never retried (bad model, auth, saturation)."""
72	
73	
74	class RateLimitError(ReasoningError):
75	    """Rate-limit / capacity signal — retried with long backpressure, not fast backoff."""
76	
77	
78	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
79	# Config (dataset-agnostic env contract, ASSAY_* prefix)
80	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
81	def _env(name: str, default: str) -> str:
82	    val = os.environ.get(name)
83	    return val if val is not None and val != "" else default
84	
85	
86	def _truthy(name: str) -> bool:
87	    return os.environ.get(name, "").strip().lower() in {"1", "true", "yes", "on"}
88	
89	
90	BULK_MODEL = _env("ASSAY_BULK_MODEL", "llama3.1:8b")
91	BULK_BASE_URL = _env("ASSAY_BULK_BASE_URL", "http://localhost:11434")
92	HIGH_STAKES_MODEL = os.environ.get("ASSAY_HIGH_STAKES_MODEL") or None
93	
94	BULK_TIMEOUT = max(1.0, float(_env("ASSAY_BULK_TIMEOUT", "120")))
95	# Cap BULK generation length. ChatOllama/Ollama default to unbounded (-1); without a cap a
96	# local model can run away to context exhaustion, and the wall-clock timeout cannot interrupt a
97	# call already blocked in the backend (a leaked pool slot). Bounding output tokens is the real
98	# guard (LangChain's own ChatOllama example sets num_predict explicitly).
99	BULK_NUM_PREDICT = max(1, int(_env("ASSAY_BULK_NUM_PREDICT", "2048")))
100	HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT = max(1.0, float(_env("ASSAY_HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT", "300")))
101	
102	MAX_RETRIES = max(0, int(_env("ASSAY_REASONING_RETRIES", "2")))
103	BACKOFF_BASE = max(0.0, float(_env("ASSAY_REASONING_BACKOFF", "1.5")))
104	RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF = max(0.0, float(_env("ASSAY_RATELIMIT_BACKOFF", "30")))
105	RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES = max(0, int(_env("ASSAY_RATELIMIT_RETRIES", "5")))
106	# run_json re-rolls on a PARSE failure. This is independent of the transient/rate-limit retry
107	# budget above (which applies per generation) so the two do not multiply into a retry storm; an
108	# overall wall-clock deadline bounds the total regardless (#102).
109	JSON_REROLLS = max(0, int(_env("ASSAY_JSON_REROLLS", "2")))
110	RUN_JSON_DEADLINE = max(1.0, float(_env("ASSAY_RUN_JSON_DEADLINE", "600")))
111	
112	# OAuth subscription token name (external CLI contract — not renamed).
113	OAUTH_TOKEN_ENV = "CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN"
114	
115	
116	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
117	# Credential scrubbing (ADR-0003 / no metered API) — pure, forward-proof
118	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
119	def is_metered_anthropic_credential(key: str) -> bool:
120	    """True for any env var the Anthropic SDK would read as a METERED credential.
121	
122	    Matches the *shape* — any ``ANTHROPIC_*`` var naming a ``KEY`` or ``TOKEN`` — rather than
123	    a static list, so a newly-added metered var cannot silently slip through. The subscription
124	    ``CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`` does not start with ``ANTHROPIC_`` and is deliberately kept.
125	
126	    The match is case-insensitive (pass 3, #F-003): a metered key set under a non-uppercase name
127	    (``anthropic_api_key``) must scrub too — the AWS_/GOOGLE_ checks already uppercase, so this
128	    closes the one credential path that did not.
129	    """
130	    ku = key.upper()
131	    return ku.startswith("ANTHROPIC_") and ("KEY" in ku or "TOKEN" in ku)
132	
133	
134	# Env vars that — beyond metered credentials — would redirect the subprocess OFF-BOX or onto a
135	# metered provider, defeating ADR-0003 / "no metered API". The claude CLI honours all of these.
136	_UNSAFE_SUBPROCESS_VARS = frozenset(
137	    {
138	        "CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK",
139	        "CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX",
140	        "CLAUDE_CODE_API_KEY_HELPER",
141	        "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS",
142	        # TLS-trust / egress-interception knobs the Node-based CLI honours: a custom CA or disabled
143	        # cert verification enables a transparent off-box MITM proxy (pass 4, #G-018).
144	        "NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS",
145	        "NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED",
146	    }
147	)
148	
149	
150	def is_unsafe_subprocess_var(key: str) -> bool:
151	    """True for any env var that must not reach the high-stakes subprocess (#101).
152	
153	    Covers metered credentials, off-box/metered-provider redirects, proxies (which redirect
154	    egress), cloud-provider credentials (AWS_*/GOOGLE_*), and TLS-trust overrides. The ENTIRE
155	    ``ANTHROPIC_*`` namespace is scrubbed (#G-018): any such var configures the SDK's endpoint /
156	    auth / headers and could redirect off-box or onto a metered provider — the subscription token
157	    is ``CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`` (no ``ANTHROPIC_`` prefix) and is deliberately NOT matched.
158	    """
159	    ku = key.upper()
160	    return (
161	        ku.startswith(
162	            "ANTHROPIC_"
163	        )  # whole namespace: endpoint/auth/headers/custom-headers (#G-018)
164	        or ku in _UNSAFE_SUBPROCESS_VARS
165	        or ku.endswith("_PROXY")  # HTTP_PROXY/HTTPS_PROXY/ALL_PROXY/NO_PROXY (any case)
166	        or ku.startswith("AWS_")
167	        or ku.startswith("GOOGLE_")
168	    )
169	
170	
171	def scrubbed_env() -> dict[str, str]:
172	    """The process env hardened for the high-stakes subprocess (ADR-0003 / no metered API).
173	
174	    Every unsafe var (:func:`is_unsafe_subprocess_var`) is **overwritten to empty**, not merely
175	    omitted. The claude-agent-sdk builds the child env as ``{**os.environ, **options.env}`` — it
176	    *merges* over the inherited environment — so a dropped key would silently fall through to its
177	    inherited value (the metered key / off-box redirect would survive). Setting it to ``""`` in
178	    the dict we hand over guarantees the merge overwrites the inherited value (#101). The
179	    subscription OAuth token is preserved so the fixed-cost path still authenticates.
180	    """
181	    env = dict(os.environ)
182	    for k in list(env):
183	        if is_unsafe_subprocess_var(k):
184	            env[k] = ""
185	    return env
186	
187	
188	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
189	# Bounded timeout pool with saturation guard
190	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
191	_POOL_WORKERS = 8
192	_pool = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=_POOL_WORKERS, thread_name_prefix="reasoning-timeout")
193	# Shut the pool down at interpreter exit (#F-043). wait=False avoids blocking exit on in-flight
194	# backend calls (already bounded by _with_timeout); without this, module globals can be torn down
195	# while a worker is mid-call, leaving behavior undefined. Mirrors checkpoint.py's atexit cleanup.
196	atexit.register(_pool.shutdown, wait=False)
197	_inflight = 0
198	_inflight_lock = threading.Lock()
199	
200	
201	def _release(future: Any) -> None:
202	    # Idempotent per future (#F-032 follow-up): decrement _inflight AT MOST ONCE for a given
203	    # future. The done-callback path and the interrupt-window fallback in _submit_bounded can BOTH
204	    # reach _release for the same future when add_done_callback appended the callback and then was
205	    # interrupted before returning — without this guard that double-decrements and over-releases a
206	    # slot (the inverse of the leak being fixed). A per-future flag set under the lock makes the
207	    # "_release runs at most once" claim true rather than aspirational.
208	    global _inflight
209	    with _inflight_lock:
210	        if getattr(future, "_assay_released", False):
211	            return
212	        try:
213	            future._assay_released = True
214	        except (AttributeError, TypeError):  # pragma: no cover - Future allows attrs; defensive
215	            pass
216	        _inflight -= 1
217	
218	
219	def _submit_bounded(fn: Callable[[], Any]) -> Any:
220	    """Submit to the shared pool, refusing if every worker slot is already in flight
221	    (hung backend calls would otherwise leak all slots)."""
222	    global _inflight
223	    with _inflight_lock:
224	        if _inflight >= _POOL_WORKERS:
225	            # Saturation is a TRANSIENT condition — surface a retryable backpressure signal so
226	            # _run_with_retries waits and re-tries rather than failing the caller permanently
227	            # (#104). Concurrent callers thus queue briefly instead of erroring out.
228	            raise RateLimitError(
229	                f"reasoning timeout pool saturated ({_inflight}/{_POOL_WORKERS} in-flight) — "
230	                "backpressure; retry shortly"
231	            )
232	        _inflight += 1
233	    try:
234	        future = _pool.submit(fn)
235	    except RuntimeError as exc:  # pool shut down at interpreter exit — normalize, don't leak (#116)
236	        with _inflight_lock:
237	            _inflight -= 1
238	        raise PermanentReasoningError(f"reasoning pool is shut down: {exc}") from exc
239	    except BaseException:
240	        with _inflight_lock:
241	            _inflight -= 1
242	        raise
243	    # Register the slot-release callback under its own guard (#F-032): if a BaseException
244	    # (KeyboardInterrupt/SystemExit) fires while attaching the callback, release the slot
245	    # explicitly so a permanent leak can't accumulate and brick the pool with spurious
246	    # RateLimitErrors. If the interrupt landed AFTER add_done_callback already appended the
247	    # callback (so it will still fire on completion), the explicit _release here and the later
248	    # callback both target this future — _release is per-future idempotent, so the slot is
249	    # released exactly once either way.
250	    try:
251	        future.add_done_callback(_release)
252	    except BaseException:
253	        _release(future)
254	        raise
255	    return future
256	
257	
258	def _with_timeout(fn: Callable[[], Any], timeout: float, what: str) -> Any:
259	    """Run a sync callable with a hard wall-clock timeout via the bounded pool."""
260	    future = _submit_bounded(fn)
261	    try:
262	        return future.result(timeout=timeout)
263	    except FuturesTimeout as exc:
264	        # No future.cancel(): a ThreadPoolExecutor cannot interrupt a thread already blocked
265	        # in a backend call. The worker keeps its slot until the call returns; the bounded
266	        # pool's saturation guard is the real protection against leaked/hung slots.
267	        raise ReasoningError(f"{what} timed out after {timeout:.0f}s") from exc

270	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
271	# JSON extraction — balanced-brace walker (robust to fences / trailing prose)
272	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
273	def _first_balanced_json(text: str) -> str | None:
274	    """Return the first balanced ``{...}`` or ``[...]`` span, respecting string literals.
275	
276	    A greedy regex grabs from the first brace to the LAST brace, mangling replies with
277	    multiple JSON values or braces in trailing prose; this walks the structure honestly.
278	    """
279	    start = None
280	    opener = closer = ""
281	    depth = 0
282	    in_string = False
283	    escape = False
284	    for i, ch in enumerate(text):
285	        if start is None:
286	            if ch in "{[":
287	                start, opener = i, ch
288	                closer = "}" if ch == "{" else "]"
289	                depth = 1
290	            continue
291	        if in_string:
292	            if escape:
293	                escape = False
294	            elif ch == "\\":
295	                escape = True
296	            elif ch == '"':
297	                in_string = False
298	            continue
299	        if ch == '"':
300	            in_string = True
301	        elif ch == opener:
302	            depth += 1
303	        elif ch == closer:
304	            depth -= 1
305	            if depth == 0:
306	                return text[start : i + 1]
307	    return None
308	
309	
310	def extract_json(text: str) -> Any:
311	    """Parse JSON from a model reply (handles code fences and surrounding prose)."""
312	    span = _first_balanced_json(text)
313	    if span is None:
314	        raise ReasoningError("no JSON object/array found in reply")
315	    try:
316	        return json.loads(span)
317	    except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
318	        raise ReasoningError(f"reply contained malformed JSON: {exc}") from exc

322	# Tier backends (lazy imports — module loads without them)
323	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
324	def _bulk_complete(
325	    prompt: str,
326	    system: str | None,
327	    temperature: float,
328	    model: str,
329	    json_mode: bool = False,
330	    *,
331	    seed: int | None = None,
332	    json_schema: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
333	) -> str:
334	    """Tier: BULK — local model runtime (loopback-enforced).
335	
336	    ``num_predict`` is always set (bounded generation). For JSON, a caller-supplied
337	    ``json_schema`` uses Ollama's schema-constrained decoding (the reliable path); otherwise
338	    ``json_mode`` falls back to loose ``format="json"``. ``seed`` lets a retry force a different
339	    deterministic decode without sharpening temperature.
340	    """
341	    require_loopback_url(BULK_BASE_URL, what="bulk-tier model base URL")
342	    try:
343	        from langchain_ollama import ChatOllama
344	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
345	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
346	            "bulk tier requires the 'reasoning' extra (langchain-ollama) — not installed"
347	        ) from exc
348	
349	    # BULK's INNER bound (#G-003): the sync-tier counterpart to HIGH_STAKES's anyio.fail_after.
350	    # A ThreadPoolExecutor worker blocked in client.invoke() cannot be interrupted by the outer
351	    # _with_timeout, so the HTTP client timeout is what actually frees the slot on a hung backend.
352	    # A bare float sets EVERY httpx phase (connect/read/write/pool) to BULK_TIMEOUT, so a slow
353	    # connect THEN a slow read could total ~2*BULK_TIMEOUT — past the outer BULK_TIMEOUT+10 bound,
354	    # leaking the slot. Use an explicit Timeout so the TOTAL stays ~BULK_TIMEOUT (+ a small connect)
355	    # and aligns with the outer headroom. Fall back to the float if httpx is absent.
356	    try:
357	        import httpx
358	
359	        _bulk_timeout: Any = httpx.Timeout(
360	            BULK_TIMEOUT, connect=min(10.0, BULK_TIMEOUT), pool=min(10.0, BULK_TIMEOUT)
361	        )
362	    except ImportError:  # pragma: no cover - httpx ships with the reasoning extra
363	        _bulk_timeout = BULK_TIMEOUT
364	    kwargs: dict[str, Any] = dict(
365	        model=model,
366	        base_url=BULK_BASE_URL,
367	        temperature=temperature,
368	        num_predict=BULK_NUM_PREDICT,  # bound generation length (H1)
369	        client_kwargs={"timeout": _bulk_timeout},
370	    )
371	    if seed is not None:
372	        kwargs["seed"] = seed
373	    if json_schema is not None:
374	        kwargs["format"] = json_schema  # Ollama schema-constrained decoding (reliable JSON)
375	    elif json_mode:
376	        kwargs["format"] = "json"  # loose native JSON decoding (syntactic only)
377	    client = ChatOllama(**kwargs)
378	    messages: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
379	    if system:
380	        messages.append(("system", system))
381	    messages.append(("human", prompt))
382	    try:
383	        reply = client.invoke(messages)
384	    except Exception as exc:
385	        # Prefer the typed HTTP status (ollama.ResponseError / httpx carry one): 404 = the model
386	        # isn't pulled → permanent. Fall back to substring matching only if no status is exposed
387	        # (robust to upstream message-wording changes — audit M4).
388	        status = getattr(exc, "status_code", None) or getattr(
389	            getattr(exc, "response", None), "status_code", None
390	        )
391	        msg = str(exc).lower()
392	        permanent = status == 404 or any(
393	            s in msg for s in ("not found", "no such model", "try pulling")
394	        )
395	        if permanent:
396	            raise PermanentReasoningError(f"bulk model {model!r} not available: {exc}") from exc
397	        raise ReasoningError(f"bulk tier call failed: {exc}") from exc
398	    content = getattr(reply, "content", reply)
399	    text = content if isinstance(content, str) else str(content)
400	    text = text.strip()
401	    if not text:  # an empty reply is not a valid result — retry, don't return "" (issue #5/R5)
402	        raise ReasoningError("bulk tier returned an empty reply")
403	    return text

408	    (the normal state after ``claude login`` without exporting a token)."""

414	def _high_stakes_complete(prompt: str, system: str | None, model: str | None) -> str:
415	    """Tier: HIGH_STAKES — frontier model via subscription CLI/Agent SDK (no metered key)."""
416	    try:
417	        import anyio
418	        from claude_agent_sdk import (
419	            AssistantMessage,
420	            ClaudeAgentOptions,
421	            ResultMessage,
422	            TextBlock,
423	            query,
424	        )
425	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
426	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
427	            "high-stakes tier requires the 'reasoning' extra (claude-agent-sdk) — not installed"
428	        ) from exc
429	    if not _high_stakes_auth_present():
430	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
431	            f"high-stakes tier requires subscription auth ({OAUTH_TOKEN_ENV} or a stored "
432	            "~/.claude/.credentials.json); none found"
433	        )
434	
435	    options = ClaudeAgentOptions(
436	        system_prompt=system,
437	        model=model,
438	        permission_mode="dontAsk",
439	        setting_sources=[],
440	        allowed_tools=[],
441	        env=scrubbed_env(),  # ADR-0003: never pass a metered Anthropic credential
442	    )
443	
444	    parts: list[str] = []
445	    result_error: dict[str, Any] | None = None
446	
447	    async def _drain() -> None:
448	        # The CLI/SDK signals rate windows and API failures by COMPLETING the stream
449	        # (a RateLimitEvent, or a terminal ResultMessage with is_error) — NOT by raising.
450	        # We must inspect the stream, not just catch exceptions (issue #R4).
451	        #
452	        # Inner cancellation (#127): wrap the stream in anyio.fail_after so a hung subprocess is
453	        # actually CANCELLED (the cancellation propagates into query() and tears down the child),
454	        # freeing the bounded-pool worker. The outer _with_timeout only raises in the caller; it
455	        # cannot cancel this thread, so without this a hung HIGH_STAKES call would leak its slot
456	        # permanently. Fires before the outer wall-clock (HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT + 30).
457	        nonlocal result_error
458	        with anyio.fail_after(HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT):
459	            await _drain_stream()
460	
461	    async def _drain_stream() -> None:
462	        nonlocal result_error
463	        async for msg in query(prompt=prompt, options=options):
464	            if type(msg).__name__ == "RateLimitEvent":
465	                rl = getattr(getattr(msg, "rate_limit_info", None), "status", None)
466	                if rl == "rejected":
467	                    result_error = {"status": 429, "detail": "RateLimitEvent rejected"}
468	                continue
469	            if getattr(msg, "status", None) == "rejected":
470	                result_error = {"status": 429, "detail": "rejected"}
471	                continue
472	            if isinstance(msg, AssistantMessage):
473	                for block in getattr(msg, "content", []) or []:
474	                    if isinstance(block, TextBlock):
475	                        parts.append(block.text)
476	            elif isinstance(msg, ResultMessage):
477	                # Do not let a trailing ResultMessage clobber an already-captured 429.
478	                if getattr(msg, "is_error", False) and not (
479	                    result_error and result_error.get("status") == 429
480	                ):
481	                    result_error = {
482	                        "status": getattr(msg, "api_error_status", None),
483	                        "detail": getattr(msg, "errors", None) or getattr(msg, "stop_reason", None),
484	                    }
485	
486	    try:
487	        anyio.run(_drain)
488	    except Exception as exc:
489	        raise ReasoningError(f"high-stakes tier call failed: {exc}") from exc
490	
491	    if result_error is not None:
492	        status = result_error.get("status")
493	        detail = result_error.get("detail") or status
494	        if status in (429, 529):
495	            raise RateLimitError(f"high-stakes tier rate window ({status}): {detail}")
496	        if status in (401, 403):
497	            raise PermanentReasoningError(f"high-stakes tier auth failed ({status}): {detail}")
498	        raise ReasoningError(f"high-stakes tier reported an error (status={status}): {detail}")
499	
500	    text = "".join(parts).strip()
501	    if not text:  # empty frontier reply is not a valid result — retry (issue #R5)
502	        raise ReasoningError("high-stakes tier returned an empty reply")
503	    return text

506	def _attempt(request: ReasoningRequest) -> str:
507	    """Route one request to its tier backend (no retry here)."""
508	    if _truthy("ASSAY_DISABLE_REASONING"):
509	        raise ReasoningError("reasoning is disabled (ASSAY_DISABLE_REASONING)")
510	    p = request.params
511	    system = p.get("system")
512	    temperature = float(p.get("temperature", 0.0))
513	    model = p.get("model")
514	    json_mode = bool(p.get("_json_mode", False))
515	    seed = p.get("_seed")
516	    json_schema = p.get("json_schema")  # optional caller schema → constrained JSON decoding
517	    if request.tier is StakesTier.BULK:
518	        return cast(
519	            str,
520	            _with_timeout(
521	                lambda: _bulk_complete(
522	                    request.prompt,
523	                    system,
524	                    temperature,
525	                    model or BULK_MODEL,
526	                    json_mode,
527	                    seed=int(seed) if seed is not None else None,
528	                    json_schema=json_schema,
529	                ),
530	                BULK_TIMEOUT + 10,
531	                "bulk tier",
532	            ),
533	        )
534	    if request.tier is StakesTier.HIGH_STAKES:
535	        # The high-stakes tier runs the Claude CLI subprocess (not the in-process Anthropic SDK,
536	        # so no auto-instrumentor covers it) — emit an explicit OpenInference LLM span so Phoenix
537	        # records the model/provider for this otherwise-untraced tier (#2).
538	        llm_attrs = {
539	            "llm.provider": "anthropic",
540	            "llm.system": "anthropic",
541	            "llm.model_name": str(model or HIGH_STAKES_MODEL or "subscription-default"),
542	        }
543	        with _span("reasoning.high_stakes", llm_attrs, kind="LLM"):
544	            return cast(
545	                str,
546	                _with_timeout(
547	                    lambda: _high_stakes_complete(
548	                        request.prompt, system, model or HIGH_STAKES_MODEL
549	                    ),
550	                    HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT + 30,
551	                    "high-stakes tier",
552	                ),
553	            )
554	    raise PermanentReasoningError(f"unknown tier: {request.tier!r}")

557	def _sleep_bounded(secs: float, deadline: float | None) -> None:
558	    """Sleep ``secs``, but never past ``deadline`` — so backoff can't blow an overall budget.
559	
560	    If the deadline has already passed, raise instead of sleeping the full interval, so a long
561	    rate-limit backoff cannot keep a caller blocked well beyond its deadline (#102).
562	    """
563	    if deadline is not None:
564	        remaining = deadline - time.monotonic()
565	        if remaining <= 0:
566	            raise ReasoningError("reasoning deadline exceeded during backoff")
567	        secs = min(secs, remaining)
568	    time.sleep(secs)
569	
570	
571	def _run_with_retries(request: ReasoningRequest, *, deadline: float | None = None) -> str:
572	    """Execute with independent transient and rate-limit retry budgets.
573	
574	    ``deadline`` (monotonic seconds) bounds both the cumulative backoff AND entry into each
575	    retry: once the deadline has passed, no further ``_attempt`` is started (#102, #F-022). It
576	    does not interrupt an attempt already in flight — that is bounded by the per-call timeout —
577	    so total wall-clock is at most ``deadline`` plus one in-flight attempt's timeout, not
578	    ``deadline`` plus the full retry budget of per-call timeouts.
579	    """
580	    transient = 0
581	    rate = 0
582	    attempted = False
583	    while True:
584	        # Gate retry ENTRY on the deadline (#F-022): the first attempt always runs, but a retry is
585	        # not started once the deadline is past — otherwise the per-attempt timeouts could stack
586	        # well beyond the overall budget even though the backoff sleeps were bounded.
587	        if attempted and deadline is not None and time.monotonic() >= deadline:
588	            raise ReasoningError("reasoning deadline exceeded before retry")
589	        attempted = True
590	        try:
591	            return _attempt(request)
592	        except PermanentReasoningError:
593	            raise
594	        except RateLimitError:
595	            if rate >= RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES:
596	                raise
597	            _sleep_bounded(RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF * (rate + 1), deadline)
598	            rate += 1
599	        except ReasoningError:
600	            if transient >= MAX_RETRIES:
601	                raise
602	            _sleep_bounded(BACKOFF_BASE**transient, deadline)
603	            transient += 1
604

611	    """Route a reasoning request to the appropriate tier with timeouts, retries, tracing."""

616	@contextmanager
617	def _span(name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any], *, kind: str = "AGENT") -> Iterator[None]:
618	    """Emit an OpenTelemetry span if a provider is registered; a cheap no-op otherwise.
619	
620	    Stamps the OpenInference ``openinference.span.kind`` (default ``AGENT`` — a reasoning call)
621	    so Phoenix classifies the span instead of rendering it UNKNOWN. OpenTelemetry is imported
622	    lazily, so the seam traces when the (self-hosted, on-box) observability stack is wired and is
623	    a silent no-op in tests / offline (ADR-0003).
624	    """
625	    try:
626	        from opentelemetry import trace
627	    except ImportError:
628	        yield
629	        return
630	    tracer = trace.get_tracer("assay_engine.reasoning")
631	    # start_as_current_span auto-records exceptions + sets ERROR status by default (#F-005 was a
632	    # false positive — see assay_engine.observability.tracing for the verification note).
633	    with tracer.start_as_current_span(name) as span:
634	        span.set_attribute("openinference.span.kind", kind)
635	        for k, v in attributes.items():
636	            span.set_attribute(k, v)
637	        yield

640	class TieredReasoningSeam:
641	    """Concrete tiered seam. ``run`` returns text; ``run_json`` parses a JSON reply.
642	
643	    Backends and tracing are resolved lazily, so an instance constructs with no external
644	    dependencies; calls fail loud (``PermanentReasoningError``) if a tier's backend or its
645	    subscription auth is absent. When the observability stack is present, each call emits an
646	    OpenTelemetry span; otherwise tracing is a no-op.
647	    """
648	
649	    def run(self, request: ReasoningRequest) -> str:
650	        attrs = {"assay.tier": request.tier.value, "assay.purpose": request.purpose}
651	        with _span("reasoning.run", attrs):
652	            return _run_with_retries(request)
653	
654	    def run_json(self, request: ReasoningRequest) -> Any:
655	        """Generate and parse JSON, re-rolling on parse failure.
656	
657	        If the caller supplies ``params["json_schema"]``, the BULK tier uses Ollama's
658	        schema-constrained decoding (the reliable path). Otherwise it falls back to loose JSON
659	        mode + prompt instruction. BULK decoding can be deterministic (temp 0), so re-issuing the
660	        identical prompt would yield a byte-identical bad reply; each retry **varies the seed**
661	        (forcing a different deterministic decode) and *monotonically* raises temperature toward
662	        1.0 — never lowering it, so re-rolls never sharpen back toward the same output. ``params``
663	        is frozen, so each attempt builds a fresh request (issue #R6).
664	        """
665	        base_temp = float(request.params.get("temperature", 0.0))
666	        system = request.params.get("system")
667	        json_system = (
668	            f"{system}\n\nRespond with ONLY a single JSON value, no prose."
669	            if system
670	            else "Respond with ONLY a single JSON value, no prose."
671	        )
672	        last: ReasoningError | None = None
673	        deadline = time.monotonic() + RUN_JSON_DEADLINE  # overall bound across all re-rolls (#102)
674	        with _span(
675	            "reasoning.run_json",
676	            {"assay.tier": request.tier.value, "assay.purpose": request.purpose},
677	        ):
678	            for attempt in range(JSON_REROLLS + 1):
679	                if attempt > 0 and time.monotonic() >= deadline:
680	                    raise last or ReasoningError("run_json exceeded its overall deadline")
681	                temp = min(1.0, base_temp + 0.2 * attempt)  # monotonic, never decreases (M3)
682	                params = dict(request.params)
683	                params.update(temperature=temp, system=json_system, _json_mode=True, _seed=attempt)
684	                attempt_req = ReasoningRequest(
685	                    prompt=request.prompt,
686	                    tier=request.tier,
687	                    purpose=request.purpose,
688	                    params=params,
689	                )
690	                # A transient/rate/permanent error is already handled (and bounded by the shared
691	                # deadline) inside _run_with_retries — let it PROPAGATE rather than re-rolling, so
692	                # the budgets do NOT multiply (#102). We re-roll ONLY on a JSON *parse* failure.
693	                text = _run_with_retries(attempt_req, deadline=deadline)
694	                try:
695	                    return extract_json(text)
696	                except ReasoningError as exc:  # parse failure → re-roll with a new seed
697	                    last = exc
698	            raise last if last is not None else ReasoningError("run_json failed")
699	
700	    @staticmethod
701	    def is_available(tier: StakesTier) -> bool:
702	        """Cheap liveness check (spends no LLM turn). Never raises — returns False on error."""
703	        if _truthy("ASSAY_DISABLE_REASONING"):
704	            return False
705	        try:
706	            if tier is StakesTier.BULK:
707	                require_loopback_url(BULK_BASE_URL, what="bulk-tier model base URL")
708	                import urllib.request
709	
710	                with urllib.request.urlopen(f"{BULK_BASE_URL}/api/tags", timeout=2) as resp:
711	                    return bool(resp.status == 200)
712	            if tier is StakesTier.HIGH_STAKES:
713	                import shutil
714	
715	                return shutil.which("claude") is not None and _high_stakes_auth_present()
716	        except Exception:
717	            return False
718	        return False

1	"""Dependency-light, deterministic numeric primitives — the baseline's raw material.
2	
3	These are the small, exact, NumPy-free building blocks the methodology calls "raw material"
4	for a baseline: similarity/distance and descriptive statistics over a
5	:class:`~assay_engine.contracts.features.FeatureMatrix`. They are deterministic (no RNG, no
6	iteration-order dependence) and pure, so a study can assemble a simple baseline — and the
7	engine can unit-test these invariants — with no heavy dependency.
8	
9	Studies needing performance or richer ML (embedding models, clustering, graph/topology) bring
10	their own builders implementing :class:`~assay_engine.baseline.toolkit.BaselineBuilder`; the
11	engine deliberately does not prescribe those choices (ADR-0002). Heavier engine helpers may be
12	added later under the optional ``baseline`` extra, but the contract here stays dependency-free.
13	"""
14	
15	from __future__ import annotations
16	
17	import math
18	from typing import Any, Sequence
19	
20	Vector = Sequence[float]
21	
22	
23	def _check_same_length(a: Vector, b: Vector) -> None:
24	    if len(a) != len(b):
25	        raise ValueError(f"vectors must have equal length; got {len(a)} and {len(b)}")
26	
27	
28	def _check_finite(*vectors: Vector) -> None:
29	    """Reject non-finite components. Without this a NaN/inf could flow into cosine and be
30	    silently clamped to a false 1.0 (audit #B6); consistent with descriptive_stats."""
31	    for vec in vectors:
32	        for x in vec:
33	            if not math.isfinite(x):
34	                raise ValueError(f"vector components must be finite; got {x}")
35	
36	
37	def dot(a: Vector, b: Vector) -> float:
38	    _check_same_length(a, b)
39	    return math.fsum(x * y for x, y in zip(a, b))
40	
41	
42	def l2_norm(a: Vector) -> float:
43	    return math.sqrt(math.fsum(x * x for x in a))
44	
45	
46	def _clamp_unit(x: float) -> float:
47	    """Clamp to [-1, 1] to absorb floating-point overshoot (e.g. self-similarity 1.0000…002)."""
48	    return max(-1.0, min(1.0, x))
49	
50	
51	def cosine_similarity(a: Vector, b: Vector) -> float:
52	    """Cosine similarity in [-1, 1]. A zero vector has no direction, so its similarity to
53	    anything is defined as 0.0 (rather than NaN). The result is clamped to [-1, 1] so float
54	    rounding cannot push it outside the documented range (audit #B3)."""
55	    _check_same_length(a, b)
56	    _check_finite(a, b)
57	    na, nb = l2_norm(a), l2_norm(b)
58	    if na == 0.0 or nb == 0.0:
59	        return 0.0
60	    return _clamp_unit(dot(a, b) / (na * nb))
61	
62	
63	def euclidean_distance(a: Vector, b: Vector) -> float:
64	    _check_same_length(a, b)
65	    _check_finite(a, b)
66	    return math.sqrt(math.fsum((x - y) ** 2 for x, y in zip(a, b)))
67	
68	
69	def _cosine_matrix_pure(rows: Sequence[Vector]) -> list[list[float]]:
70	    """Pure-Python reference implementation — O(n²·d). Correct but slow; the public function
71	    dispatches to a numpy path at scale when available."""
72	    n = len(rows)
73	    _check_finite(*rows)
74	    norms = [l2_norm(r) for r in rows]
75	    out = [[0.0] * n for _ in range(n)]
76	    for i in range(n):
77	        for j in range(i, n):
78	            if norms[i] == 0.0 or norms[j] == 0.0:
79	                sim = 0.0
80	            elif i == j:
81	                sim = 1.0  # a non-zero vector's self-similarity is exactly 1.0 (no float drift)
82	            else:
83	                sim = _clamp_unit(dot(rows[i], rows[j]) / (norms[i] * norms[j]))
84	            out[i][j] = sim
85	            out[j][i] = sim
86	    return out
87	
88	
89	def _cosine_ndarray(rows: Sequence[Vector]) -> Any:
90	    """Compute the cosine matrix as a numpy ndarray (no nested-list boxing)."""
91	    import numpy as np
92	
93	    x = np.asarray(rows, dtype=float)
94	    if x.ndim != 2:
95	        raise ValueError("rows must be a 2-D collection of equal-length vectors")
96	    if not np.isfinite(x).all():
97	        raise ValueError("vector components must be finite")
98	    norms = np.linalg.norm(x, axis=1)
99	    if not np.isfinite(norms).all():
100	        # finite inputs can still overflow when squared (||x||) on huge-magnitude vectors;
101	        # reject rather than silently produce inf/inf similarities (audit N1; restores parity
102	        # with the pure path, which raises on the same input).
103	        raise ValueError("vector norm overflow — component magnitudes are too large")
104	    zero = norms == 0.0
105	    safe = np.where(zero, 1.0, norms)
106	    xn = x / safe[:, None]
107	    m = xn @ xn.T
108	    np.clip(m, -1.0, 1.0, out=m)
109	    if zero.any():  # zero vectors have no direction → similarity 0 to everything
110	        m[zero, :] = 0.0
111	        m[:, zero] = 0.0
112	    np.fill_diagonal(m, np.where(zero, 0.0, 1.0))
113	    return m
114	
115	
116	def _cosine_matrix_numpy(rows: Sequence[Vector]) -> list[list[float]]:
117	    return [[float(v) for v in row] for row in _cosine_ndarray(rows)]
118	
119	
120	def cosine_similarity_matrix_array(rows: Sequence[Vector]) -> Any:
121	    """The cosine matrix as a numpy ndarray — no nested-list boxing (#106).
122	
123	    For large corpora the nested-list form of :func:`cosine_similarity_matrix` multiplies peak
124	    memory several-fold; a builder operating at scale should keep the ndarray and store it in the
125	    baseline's ``contents``. The determinism freeze still copies the array's bytes once
126	    (``freeze`` -> ``tobytes()`` into a ``(kind, shape, bytes)`` descriptor, O(size)) — far cheaper
127	    than freezing a deep nested list element-by-element, but NOT zero-copy/O(1) (pass 4, #G-024).
128	    Requires numpy (the ``baseline`` extra).
129	    """
130	    try:
131	        import numpy as np
132	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
133	        raise RuntimeError(
134	            "cosine_similarity_matrix_array requires numpy (the 'baseline' extra)"
135	        ) from exc
136	    if not rows:
137	        return np.empty((0, 0), dtype=float)
138	    return _cosine_ndarray(rows)
139	
140	
141	def cosine_similarity_matrix(rows: Sequence[Vector]) -> list[list[float]]:
142	    """Full symmetric cosine-similarity matrix (diagonal 1.0 for non-zero vectors, 0.0 for zero).
143	
144	    Pure-Python is O(n²·d) and does not scale (a corpus of thousands of embeddings would take
145	    minutes-to-hours); when numpy is available (the optional ``baseline`` extra) this dispatches
146	    to a vectorized path that is orders of magnitude faster. The two paths agree to within
147	    floating-point tolerance (both clamp to [-1, 1]); a study needing byte-identical
148	    reproducibility should pin its environment (the determinism harness records component
149	    versions for exactly this reason).
150	    """
151	    if not rows:
152	        return []
153	    try:
154	        import numpy  # noqa: F401
155	    except ImportError:
156	        return _cosine_matrix_pure(rows)
157	    return _cosine_matrix_numpy(rows)
158	
159	
160	def _quantile(sorted_values: list[float], q: float) -> float:
161	    """Linear-interpolation quantile (``q`` in [0, 1]) over already-sorted values."""
162	    if not 0.0 <= q <= 1.0:
163	        raise ValueError(f"quantile q must be in [0, 1]; got {q}")
164	    n = len(sorted_values)
165	    if n == 1:
166	        return sorted_values[0]
167	    pos = q * (n - 1)
168	    lo = math.floor(pos)
169	    hi = math.ceil(pos)
170	    if lo == hi:
171	        return sorted_values[lo]
172	    frac = pos - lo
173	    return sorted_values[lo] * (1 - frac) + sorted_values[hi] * frac
174	
175	
176	def descriptive_stats(values: Sequence[float]) -> dict[str, float]:
177	    """Descriptive statistics of ``values``.
178	
179	    Returns ``n`` (count), ``mean``, ``std`` (sample std, ddof=1; ``0.0`` for a single value
180	    rather than NaN), ``min``, ``max``, and ``q25``/``median``/``q75`` (linear-interpolation
181	    quantiles). Raises on an empty input or any non-finite value.
182	    """
183	    if not values:
184	        raise ValueError("descriptive_stats requires at least one value")
185	    n = len(values)
186	    for v in values:
187	        if not math.isfinite(v):
188	            raise ValueError(f"values must be finite; got {v}")
189	    mean = math.fsum(values) / n
190	    if n == 1:
191	        std = 0.0
192	    else:
193	        var = math.fsum((v - mean) ** 2 for v in values) / (n - 1)
194	        std = math.sqrt(var)
195	    s = sorted(values)
196	    return {
197	        "n": float(n),
198	        "mean": mean,
199	        "std": std,
200	        "min": s[0],
201	        "max": s[-1],
202	        "q25": _quantile(s, 0.25),
203	        "median": _quantile(s, 0.5),
204	        "q75": _quantile(s, 0.75),
205	    }
206

270	    secret: bytes | None = None,
271	    trail: ProvenanceTrail | None = None,
272	    verify_trail: bool = True,
273	) -> StudyResult:
274	    """Run one study end-to-end through its governed phase sequence. See module docstring.
275	
276	    ``gate_handler`` is **required** (no default), so governance review is a conscious choice —
277	    pass :func:`auto_approve` to opt out of human review explicitly, or an operator handler that
278	    can block/park. ``tracer`` defaults to :class:`OtelTracer` (real spans if OpenTelemetry + a
279	    provider are present, a graceful no-op otherwise). ``tracker`` (optional) logs params/metrics
280	    to an experiment store, best-effort (a tracker failure never aborts the run). ``versioner``
281	    (optional) content-addresses the source so the trail cites retrievable bytes. ``clock``
282	    injects a deterministic clock for the provenance timestamps only (ordering checks always use
283	    real time). ``secret`` keys the internal provenance trail with HMAC (forgery-resistant);
284	    mutually exclusive with passing your own ``trail``. ``trail`` may be a caller-owned
285	    :class:`ProvenanceTrail` so a run that *raises* still leaves the caller the partial trail.
286	
287	    Raises ``IngestionError`` on a bad source, ``FirewallViolation``/``PreRegistrationError`` on
288	    a methodological breach, ``GateError`` if a gate blocks, and ``ValueError`` on a malformed run.
289	    """
290	    tracer = tracer or OtelTracer()
291	    if trail is not None and secret is not None:
292	        raise ValueError("pass either a caller-owned trail OR a secret to key a new one, not both")
293	    if secret is None and trail is None:
294	        # An engine-created unkeyed trail is tamper-evident (a naive edit breaks the SHA-256
295	        # chain) but NOT forgery-resistant: an adversary with write access can edit an entry and
296	        # recompute the whole genesis-rooted chain. That is fine for local reproducibility but
297	        # insufficient for an audit trail that crosses a trust boundary. Flag the insecure
298	        # default rather than letting it pass silently (pass 3, #F-049); pass a `secret` to key
299	        # the trail with HMAC, or `clock=`-driven deterministic repro if forgery is out of scope.
300	        warnings.warn(
301	            "run_study provenance trail is UNKEYED (secret=None) — tamper-evident but not "
302	            "forgery-resistant. Pass secret=<bytes> to HMAC-key the trail for audit trails that "
303	            "cross a trust boundary.",
304	            stacklevel=2,
305	        )
306	    defn = plan.definition
307	    modes = defn.modes
308	    required = required_phases(modes)
309	    trail = trail if trail is not None else ProvenanceTrail(secret=secret, clock=clock)
310	    visited: list[Phase] = []
311	    run_id: str | None = None
312	
313	    def enter(phase: Phase, **payload: Any) -> None:
314	        visited.append(phase)
315	        trail.record("phase", f"enter {phase.name}", phase=phase.name, **payload)
316	
317	    def track(fn: Callable[[ExperimentTracker, str], None]) -> None:
318	        # observability is best-effort: a tracker failure is recorded but never aborts a run.
319	        if tracker is None or run_id is None:
320	            return
321	        try:
322	            fn(tracker, run_id)
323	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — tracker is an external, optional backend
324	            trail.record("tracking_error", f"experiment tracker call failed: {exc}")
325	
326	    def run_gate(review: GateReview) -> None:
327	        # Record the decision once (snapshot approved/reason) and block on rejection (#94).
328	        decision = gate_handler(review)
329	        approved = bool(getattr(decision, "approved", False))
330	        reason = str(getattr(decision, "reason", ""))
331	        trail.record(
332	            "gate",
333	            f"gate {review.gate!r}: {'approved' if approved else 'blocked'}",
334	            gate=review.gate,
335	            approved=approved,
336	            reason=reason,
337	            transition=f"{review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}",
338	        )
339	        if not approved:
340	            raise GateError(
341	                f"gate {review.gate!r} blocked {review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}: {reason}"
342	            )
343	
344	    trail.record(
345	        "run_start",
346	        f"study {defn.name!r} starting",
347	        study=defn.name,
348	        modes=sorted(m.value for m in modes),
349	        research_questions=list(defn.research_questions),
350	    )
351	    if tracker is not None:
352	        try:
353	            run_id = tracker.start_run(
354	                defn.name,
355	                {
356	                    "modes": ",".join(sorted(m.value for m in modes)),
357	                    "n_research_questions": len(defn.research_questions),
358	                },
359	            )
360	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
361	            # run_id stays None → every track() call silently no-ops and ALL metrics are dropped
362	            # for the whole run. Record it to the trail AND emit a structured warning so an
363	            # operator seeing no MLflow run knows why instead of guessing (pass 3, #F-028).
364	            trail.record("tracking_error", f"experiment tracker start_run failed: {exc}")
365	            _log.warning(
366	                "experiment tracker start_run failed (%s: %s); run metrics will not be logged "
367	                "to the tracker for this study",
368	                type(exc).__name__,
369	                exc,
370	            )
371	
372	    ok = False
373	    started_at = time.monotonic()
374	    trace_ctx = ExitStack()
375	    try:
376	        # Bootstrap tracing once and correlate every phase/reasoning span to this run via baggage,
377	        # so a run's spans are findable by id and join the experiment-tracker run (#122).
378	        # Done INSIDE the try so a tracing-setup failure is still covered by the finally's
379	        # tracker.end_run cleanup — an already-started tracker run must never leak in RUNNING
380	        # state (the #110 class of bug) if bootstrap/context entry raises (#156). Best-effort.
381	        bootstrap_tracing()
382	        corr_id = run_id or uuid.uuid4().hex
383	        trace_ctx.enter_context(run_trace_context(corr_id))
384	        # ---- INGEST ----
385	        with tracer.span("phase:INGEST"):
386	            enter(Phase.INGEST)
387	            try:
388	                corpus = defn.parser.parse(plan.source)
389	                source_fp = defn.parser.source_fingerprint(plan.source)
390	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — normalize adapter/IO ingestion failures
391	                raise IngestionError(f"parser failed to ingest {plan.source!r}: {exc}") from exc
392	            # The parser Protocol return type is unenforced at runtime; a misimplemented adapter
393	            # that returns None/list/dict would otherwise slip past the normalizer above and die
394	            # at `corpus.units` with an opaque AttributeError, violating the documented
395	            # "Raises IngestionError on a bad source" contract (#134).
396	            if not isinstance(corpus, Corpus):
397	                raise IngestionError(f"parser returned {type(corpus).__name__}, expected Corpus")
398	            if not isinstance(source_fp, str):
399	                raise IngestionError(
400	                    f"parser.source_fingerprint returned {type(source_fp).__name__}, expected str"
401	                )
402	            if not corpus.units:
403	                raise IngestionError("ingestion produced an empty corpus")
404	            cfp = corpus_fingerprint(corpus)
405	            source_version: str | None = None
406	            if versioner is not None:
407	                # The versioner is an optional seam; a storage failure (PermissionError,
408	                # OSError) must surface as the documented IngestionError, not a raw untyped
409	                # exception that the caller cannot distinguish from a fatal firewall breach
410	                # (pass 3, #F-008). Versioning is part of ingesting the source, so a failure to
411	                # content-address it is an ingestion failure.
412	                try:
413	                    source_version = versioner.put(str(plan.source))
414	                except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — normalize optional-seam storage failures
415	                    raise IngestionError(
416	                        f"data versioner failed to store {plan.source!r}: {exc}"
417	                    ) from exc
418	            trail.record(
419	                "ingest",
420	                "parsed source into canonical corpus",
421	                source_fingerprint=source_fp,
422	                corpus_fingerprint=cfp,
423	                n_units=len(corpus.units),
424	                n_relations=len(corpus.relations),
425	                source_version=source_version,
426	            )
427	            # SLO-relevant scale metrics to the tracker so corpus-size↔latency regressions are
428	            # analysable from the experiment store, not only the provenance trail (pass 3,
429	            # #F-029). Best-effort via track() (no-ops if no tracker/run_id).

1	"""Durable run-state checkpointer — local, data-sovereign (ADR-0003).
2	
3	The orchestration graph persists its full state (current phase, gate decisions, locked
4	hypotheses, verdicts) through a checkpointer so a run can interrupt at a human-approval gate
5	and resume later. That state holds the entire analysis, so the store is **on-box only**
6	(ADR-0003); a SaaS checkpointer is disqualified.
7	
8	This module is the LangGraph checkpointer factory, ported faithfully from the prior
9	platform's hardened implementation and generalized (dataset-agnostic ``ASSAY_*`` env, no
10	config-file coupling — see ADR-0006). The hardened behaviors are deliberately preserved
11	because each prevents a real production failure:
12	
13	- a **self-healing ConnectionPool** (not a raw connection): when Postgres goes away — node
14	  sleep, restart, idle timeout, a network blip — a cached raw connection is permanently
15	  broken and every later checkpoint op raises, i.e. durability is lost exactly during the
16	  interrupt/resume the checkpointer exists to support. The pool runs a liveness check on
17	  checkout and replaces dead connections;
18	- a **session advisory lock** serializing first-time schema setup across *processes*, run on
19	  the *same* connection as the DDL (a pool would otherwise check out a different connection,
20	  leaving the lock ineffective);
21	- a **once-per-process-per-conn guard** (``setup()`` is idempotent but re-locking + replaying
22	  the migration query on every call is wasteful), with an in-process lock making the
23	  check-and-set atomic so concurrent threads don't both run setup;
24	- **credential redaction**: a backend exception (psycopg/psycopg_pool) can embed the full
25	  ``user:password`` URI/DSN in its own message, so the conn string is scrubbed precisely and
26	  the exception text via URI-userinfo + DSN-password backstops; the redacted message is then
27	  raised *outside* the exception handler, so the credential-bearing original is retained as
28	  neither ``__cause__`` nor ``__context__`` (a logger walking ``__context__`` cannot
29	  re-expose it);
30	- a single shared **atexit** pool-cleanup handler (one per process, not one per call) so the
31	  pool's worker thread is joined before interpreter finalization.
32	
33	LangGraph/psycopg are optional (the ``persistence`` extra) and imported lazily; the
34	credential-redaction and connection-resolution logic is pure and unit-tested offline.
35	"""
36	
37	from __future__ import annotations
38	
39	import atexit
40	import os
41	import re
42	import threading
43	from typing import Any, cast
44	from urllib.parse import urlsplit
45	
46	from assay_engine._local import require_local_uri
47	
48	_DEFAULT_PG = "postgresql://localhost:5432/assay"
49	
50	# Arbitrary fixed key for the Postgres session advisory lock that serializes checkpoint
51	# schema migrations across concurrent initializers. The value is irrelevant beyond being a
52	# stable per-logical-schema constant; "ASSY" as an int.
53	_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY = 0x4153_5359
54	
55	# Upper bound (milliseconds) on how long the cross-process advisory lock may be waited for
56	# during schema bootstrap. A stuck/crashed peer that still holds a live PG session on the lock
57	# must NOT be able to hang bootstrap forever (#129); lock_timeout aborts the wait. 0 disables
58	# the bound (block indefinitely, the pre-#129 behavior). Read fresh on each bootstrap (#F-033) so
59	# an env var set after import still takes effect; the module constant is the default + the value
60	# tests monkeypatch.
61	_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS = max(0, int(os.environ.get("ASSAY_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS", "30000")))
62	
63	
64	def _migration_lock_timeout_ms() -> int:
65	    """The advisory-lock wait bound, re-read from the env each call (#F-033).
66	
67	    Falls back to the import-time module constant when the env var is unset, so a test that
68	    monkeypatches ``_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS`` still drives the value.
69	    """
70	    raw = os.environ.get("ASSAY_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS")
71	    if raw is None:
72	        return _MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS
73	    return max(0, int(raw))
74	
75	
76	# Brief meta-lock guarding the process-local registries below (atexit registration, the open-pool
77	# list, the per-conn lock map, the pool cache). It is held ONLY for in-memory check-and-set — it
78	# is NEVER held across blocking cross-process I/O (pool.connection(), the advisory-lock wait, or
79	# setup() DDL). That blocking work is serialized per conn_str instead (#129), so a stuck advisory
80	# lock on one database cannot block initialization of an unrelated one.
81	_init_lock = threading.Lock()
82	
83	# Per-conn_str bootstrap locks: only same-conn_str initializers serialize; different databases
84	# bootstrap concurrently. Created lazily under _init_lock.
85	_CONN_INIT_LOCKS: dict[str, threading.Lock] = {}
86	
87	# Live pools cached by conn_str so the factory reuses one self-healing pool (and its worker
88	# thread) per database rather than opening a fresh pool on every call (#144).
89	_POOLS_BY_CONN: dict[str, Any] = {}
90	
91	# Pools opened by the factory, closed once at process exit via a single shared atexit handler
92	# (registering one handler per call would accumulate handlers across repeated factory calls).
93	_OPEN_POOLS: list[Any] = []
94	_atexit_registered = False
95	
96	# Strips ``://user:password@`` (URI userinfo) from any URI-like substring. '/' is allowed
97	# inside the userinfo so a password containing '/' is still redacted; only '@' (the userinfo
98	# terminator) and whitespace bound the match.
99	_CREDS_RE = re.compile(r"://[^@\s]+@")
100	# Strips a libpq keyword/value DSN password token (``password=...`` / ``pgpassword=...``),
101	# handling quoted values that may contain spaces.
102	_DSN_PW_RE = re.compile(r"(?i)\b(password|pgpassword)\s*=\s*('[^']*'|\"[^\"]*\"|\S+)")
103	
104	
105	def _sanitize_conn_str(conn_str: str) -> str:
106	    """Return ``scheme://host[:port]/path`` with any ``user:password`` userinfo stripped.
107	
108	    Robust to malformed userinfo: a password containing ``/``/``#`` makes ``urlsplit``'s
109	    ``.port`` raise ``ValueError`` on access, so the whole parse (including attribute access)
110	    is guarded and falls back to the generic userinfo-strip regex / a constant placeholder.
111	    """
112	    try:
113	        u = urlsplit(conn_str)
114	        host = u.hostname or ""
115	        port = u.port
116	        scheme = u.scheme
117	        path = u.path
118	    except ValueError:
119	        return _CREDS_RE.sub("://", conn_str)
120	    if not host:
121	        return _CREDS_RE.sub("://", conn_str) if scheme else "<store>"
122	    if ":" in host:  # IPv6 literal — urlsplit drops the brackets; restore them
123	        host = f"[{host}]"
124	    netloc = f"{host}:{port}" if port else host
125	    return f"{scheme}://{netloc}{path}" if scheme else "<store>"
126	
127	
128	def redact_creds(text: str, conn_str: str = "") -> str:
129	    """Remove credentials a backend exception may embed in its message text.
130	
131	    Layers, in order of precision:
132	
133	    1. exact replacement of the known ``conn_str`` (handles any password characters, incl.
134	       ``/`` and spaces) — this is the precise layer and covers the real connection string;
135	    2. a generic URI-userinfo strip (``://user:pass@`` → ``://``) as a backstop for any
136	       *other* URI in the text;
137	    3. a libpq DSN ``password=``/``pgpassword=`` strip for the keyword/value form.
138	
139	    Layers 2–3 are best-effort for *secondary* endpoints (e.g. a replica DSN) the caller did
140	    not pass as ``conn_str``; the primary connection string is always scrubbed precisely.
141	    """
142	    if conn_str:
143	        text = text.replace(conn_str, _sanitize_conn_str(conn_str))
144	    text = _CREDS_RE.sub("://", text)
145	    return _DSN_PW_RE.sub(r"\1=***", text)
146	
147	
148	def get_postgres_connection_string() -> str:
149	    """Resolve the Postgres connection string (``ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL`` env → loopback default).
150	
151	    The env path bypasses any external config validation, so loopback is enforced here at the
152	    point of use (ADR-0003 defense-in-depth): graph state must not leave the box.
153	    """
154	    url = os.environ.get("ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL") or _DEFAULT_PG
155	    return require_local_uri(url, what="checkpointer connection")
156	
157	
158	def _safe_close(pool: Any) -> None:
159	    """Close one pool, swallowing errors so a single failing ``close()`` does not abort
160	    cleanup of the remaining pools at interpreter exit (``close()`` calls a C ``finish()``
161	    that can raise)."""
162	    try:
163	        pool.close()
164	    except Exception:
165	        pass
166	
167	
168	def _close_all_pools() -> None:
169	    # Snapshot under the meta-lock before iterating (#F-020): _OPEN_POOLS is appended under
170	    # _init_lock elsewhere, and on a free-threaded (GIL-less) build a concurrent list.append
171	    # reallocation during iteration is undefined behavior. _safe_close never takes _init_lock, so
172	    # closing outside the lock cannot deadlock.
173	    with _init_lock:
174	        pools = list(_OPEN_POOLS)
175	    for p in pools:
176	        _safe_close(p)
177	
178	
179	def _register_pool_cleanup(pool: Any) -> None:
180	    global _atexit_registered
181	    with _init_lock:
182	        _OPEN_POOLS.append(pool)
183	        if not _atexit_registered:
184	            atexit.register(_close_all_pools)
185	            _atexit_registered = True
186	
187	
188	def _conn_init_lock(conn_str: str) -> threading.Lock:
189	    """Return the per-conn_str bootstrap lock, creating it under the brief meta-lock (#129)."""
190	    with _init_lock:
191	        lock = _CONN_INIT_LOCKS.get(conn_str)
192	        if lock is None:
193	            lock = _CONN_INIT_LOCKS[conn_str] = threading.Lock()
194	        return lock
195	
196	
197	def _cached_pool(conn_str: str) -> Any:
198	    with _init_lock:
199	        return _POOLS_BY_CONN.get(conn_str)
200	
201	
202	def _acquire_migration_lock(conn: Any) -> None:
203	    """Acquire the cross-process schema-migration advisory lock with a bounded wait (#129).
204	
205	    ``lock_timeout`` aborts the ``pg_advisory_lock`` wait if a peer holds it past the deadline,
206	    so a stuck/crashed initializer cannot hang bootstrap indefinitely; the resulting error
207	    propagates as a redacted ``RuntimeError`` like any other backend failure. set_config is used
208	    (not ``SET``) so the value can be safely parameterized.
209	    """
210	    timeout_ms = _migration_lock_timeout_ms()
211	    if timeout_ms:
212	        conn.execute("SELECT set_config('lock_timeout', %s, false)", (str(timeout_ms),))
213	    conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_lock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
214	
215	
216	def get_checkpointer(use_memory: bool = False) -> Any:
217	    """Return a configured LangGraph checkpointer.
218	
219	    ``use_memory=True`` returns an in-memory saver (tests / no-Postgres). Otherwise a
220	    Postgres-backed saver over a self-healing pool, with advisory-locked one-time schema
221	    setup. Raises ``RuntimeError`` (with credentials redacted) if the backend is unreachable
222	    or the extra is not installed.
223	    """
224	    try:
225	        if use_memory:
226	            from langgraph.checkpoint.memory import MemorySaver
227	
228	            return MemorySaver()
229	        from langgraph.checkpoint.postgres import PostgresSaver
230	        from psycopg.rows import dict_row
231	        from psycopg_pool import ConnectionPool
232	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
233	        raise RuntimeError(
234	            "Postgres checkpointer requires the 'persistence' extra "
235	            "(langgraph-checkpoint-postgres, psycopg[binary,pool]) — not installed"
236	        ) from exc
237	
238	    conn_str = get_postgres_connection_string()
239	
240	    # Fast path: reuse a live, self-healing pool for this conn_str rather than opening a new pool
241	    # (and its background worker thread) on every call (#144). The liveness check on checkout
242	    # replaces dead connections, so a cached pool stays valid across transient outages.
243	    cached = _cached_pool(conn_str)
244	    if cached is not None:
245	        return PostgresSaver(cast(Any, cached))
246	
247	    # Serialize bootstrap PER conn_str — NOT process-globally (#129). Unrelated databases
248	    # initialize concurrently, and a stuck advisory lock on one conn_str can never block init of
249	    # another. The brief meta-lock (_init_lock) is only taken inside the helpers, never held
250	    # across the blocking advisory-lock wait / DDL below.
251	    with _conn_init_lock(conn_str):
252	        cached = _cached_pool(conn_str)  # another thread may have built it while we waited
253	        if cached is not None:
254	            return PostgresSaver(cast(Any, cached))
255	
256	        failure: str | None = None
257	        pool: Any = None
258	        try:
259	            # Pool bounds are env-tunable (#F-040): the reasoning seam already runs 8 worker
260	            # threads, so a fixed max_size=8 can be saturated under concurrent reasoning+checkpoint
261	            # load on larger hardware. connect_timeout bounds an individual TCP connect so a
262	            # black-holed host fails fast instead of hanging bootstrap for the OS default.
263	            min_size = max(0, int(os.environ.get("ASSAY_POOL_MIN_SIZE", "1")))
264	            max_size = max(min_size or 1, int(os.environ.get("ASSAY_POOL_MAX_SIZE", "8")))
265	            connect_timeout = max(1, int(os.environ.get("ASSAY_POOL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT", "10")))
266	            pool = ConnectionPool(
267	                conn_str,
268	                min_size=min_size,
269	                max_size=max_size,
270	                kwargs={
271	                    "autocommit": True,
272	                    "prepare_threshold": 0,
273	                    "row_factory": dict_row,
274	                    "connect_timeout": connect_timeout,
275	                },
276	                check=ConnectionPool.check_connection,  # liveness check on checkout (self-healing)
277	                open=True,
278	            )
279	            _register_pool_cleanup(pool)
280	            # Bootstrap the schema once per process per conn_str. The advisory lock and the DDL
281	            # must run on the SAME connection (pg_advisory_lock is session-scoped); a pool-backed
282	            # setup() would lock one connection and DDL on another, leaving the lock ineffective.
283	            with pool.connection() as conn:
284	                _acquire_migration_lock(conn)  # bounded wait so a stuck peer can't hang us (#129)
285	                try:
286	                    # row_factory=dict_row makes this connection dict-rowed at runtime
287	                    # (what PostgresSaver requires); the cast asserts that runtime truth
288	                    # the type system can't infer from the pool kwargs.
289	                    PostgresSaver(cast(Any, conn)).setup()  # DDL on the locked connection
290	                finally:
291	                    # Best-effort unlock; the lock auto-releases when the connection
292	                    # returns to the pool, and raising here would mask a setup() error.
293	                    try:
294	                        conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_unlock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
295	                    except Exception:
296	                        pass
297	                    # Reset the session lock_timeout GUC (#G-002): the autocommit connection set it
298	                    # at SESSION scope for the bootstrap, then returns to the pool and is reused for
299	                    # all later checkpoint reads/writes — which would otherwise silently inherit a
300	                    # non-default lock_timeout the operator never configured for normal ops.
301	                    try:
302	                        conn.execute("RESET lock_timeout")
303	                    except Exception:
304	                        pass
305	            with _init_lock:
306	                _POOLS_BY_CONN[conn_str] = pool  # cache for reuse on later calls (#144)
307	            return PostgresSaver(cast(Any, pool))  # dict-rowed pool (row_factory=dict_row)
308	        except Exception as exc:
309	            # Build the redacted message here, but raise it OUTSIDE the handler (below) so the
310	            # original — credential-bearing — exception is retained as neither __cause__ NOR
311	            # __context__ (a logger walking __context__ would otherwise re-expose the password).
312	            failure = (
313	                f"Postgres checkpointer connection failed (is Postgres running on "
314	                f"{_sanitize_conn_str(conn_str)}?): {redact_creds(str(exc), conn_str)}. "
315	                "For tests without Postgres, use get_checkpointer(use_memory=True)."
316	            )
317	            # The pool may have opened before bootstrap failed — close it and drop it from the
318	            # cleanup registry so a failed init does not leak the connection/worker thread (#105).
319	            if pool is not None:
320	                _safe_close(pool)
321	                with _init_lock:
322	                    if pool in _OPEN_POOLS:
323	                        _OPEN_POOLS.remove(pool)
324	        raise RuntimeError(failure)
325	
326	
327	def configured_checkpointer(use_memory: bool | None = None) -> Any:
328	    """Return the checkpointer the environment selects.
329	
330	    ``use_memory`` (or ``ASSAY_CHECKPOINT_BACKEND=memory``) chooses the in-memory saver;
331	    otherwise Postgres.
332	    """
333	    if use_memory is None:
334	        use_memory = os.environ.get("ASSAY_CHECKPOINT_BACKEND", "postgres").lower() == "memory"
335	    return get_checkpointer(use_memory=use_memory)
336
